title: hieracium caesium subsp. macrocalyx
intro: Hieracium caesium subsp. macrocalyx, also known as the greater hawk's beard, is a perennial plant species in the dandelion family (Asteraceae) characterized by its bright yellow flowers and coarse, hairy leaves. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been attributed to its high concentration of flavonoids and terpenoids. The health benefits of Hieracium caesium subsp. macrocalyx include its potential to reduce inflammation and improve digestion, as well as its use as a natural remedy for skin conditions and wounds. The plant is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be made by steeping the leaves and flowers in hot water or a solvent like ethanol.
